meta.title: PoSERS — Post-Stroke Epilepsy Risk Scale
meta.authors: Strzelczyk A, Haag A, Raupach H, et al.
meta.outcome: Risk of epilepsy after stroke, from a 7-item clinical scale (PoSERS)
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. The Post-Stroke Epilepsy Risk Scale (PoSERS) (Strzelczyk et al., J Neurol 2010) is a points scale for the risk of epilepsy after stroke. Prospective single-centre cohort (264 patients, 10 developed epilepsy; overall 1-year epilepsy risk 3.8%).

How to read it

  • Sum the items (range 0–10). The authors defined cut-offs rather than a per-score risk: a score ≤5 → low risk (very high negative predictive value); 6 → ~56%, 7 → ~67%, ≥8 → very high probability of epilepsy (positive predictive values).

Caution: these figures come from a small cohort with only 10 epilepsy cases, so the high-score probabilities are unstable (the paper's 100% at the top cut-offs reflects zero false-positives in a tiny sample, not a true 100% risk). Demonstration tool only.

predictors.name: Supratentorial stroke
predictors.assess: Stroke located above the tentorium. 2 points.

predictors.name: ICH involving cortical areas
predictors.assess: Intracerebral haemorrhage involving the cortex. 2 points.

predictors.name: Late seizure (≥15 days after stroke)
predictors.assess: A seizure 15 days or more after the stroke. 2 points. (Hide this in early bedside use — see note.)

predictors.name: Ischaemia involving cortical / cortical-subcortical areas
predictors.assess: Ischaemic stroke involving cortical or cortico-subcortical territory. 1 point.

predictors.name: Ischaemia with ongoing neurological deficit (>24 h)
Early seizure (≤14 days after stroke)
predictors.assess: A seizure within 14 days of the stroke. 1 point.

model.note: Small single-centre cohort (264 patients, 10 epilepsy cases) — risk figures are cut-off positive-predictive-values and are unstable at high scores. ≤5 = low risk (NPV ~100%); 6 ≈ 56%; 7 ≈ 67%; ≥8 = very high (paper reports ~100%, shown here as >90% given the tiny sample). An early-bedside version omits the late-seizure item (range 0–8).

provenance.source: Strzelczyk 2010 J Neurol Table 1 (items) + Table 3 (cut-off Sn/Sp/PPV/NPV) — doi 10.1007/s00415-010-5520-9, PMID 20309571
provenance.notes: PoSERS final scale 0-10: supratentorial 2, ICH-cortical 2, late-seizure(≥15d) 2, ischaemia-cortical 1, ischaemia+deficit 1, mRS≥3 1, early-seizure(≤14d) 1. No published score→risk table; risk approximated from cut-off PPV (≥6→55.6%, ≥7→66.6%, ≥8→100% capped to >90%), ≤5→low via NPV~100%. Small cohort (n=264, 10 events) — WEAK/unstable, flag. base rate 3.8%. Built 2026-07-08.
